facets.title: Leather jacket
facets.subtitle: Broken-in black leather biker jacket with a worn collar and heavy zip.

curator.name: Noor B.
curator.tone: Scrappy thrift enthusiast seeking stories in style pieces!
curator.bio: A thrift devotee digging the city's flea markets and second-hand rails for pieces with a real story.
curator.quote: The best pieces already have a past. I just give them a second act.
curator.antwerpTips: For everyday vintage, go to Think Twice and Episode on Kammenstraat. For older and rarer pieces, walk along Kloosterstraat. Visit on a Friday for the Vrijdagmarkt flea market, or a Sunday for the brocante market at Sint-Annaplein.

facets.title: Vintage windbreaker
facets.subtitle: Colour-block 90s shell windbreaker, boxy fit, fully packable.

curator.name: Noor B.
curator.tone: Scrappy thrift enthusiast seeking stories in style pieces!
curator.bio: A thrift devotee digging the city's flea markets and second-hand rails for pieces with a real story.
curator.quote: The best pieces already have a past. I just give them a second act.
curator.antwerpTips: For everyday vintage, go to Think Twice and Episode on Kammenstraat. For older and rarer pieces, walk along Kloosterstraat. Visit on a Friday for the Vrijdagmarkt flea market, or a Sunday for the brocante market at Sint-Annaplein.

facets.title: Pleated midi skirt
facets.subtitle: Knife-pleated midi in a muted check, sits high on the waist.

curator.name: Vincent D.
curator.tone: Vibrant, loud & structural streetwear style
curator.bio: Streetwear with an artist's eye. Loud, structural silhouettes pulled from Antwerp's boutiques and skate spots.
curator.quote: If it does not make you look twice on the street, it is not worth wearing.
curator.antwerpTips: Kammenstraat is the main street for streetwear. Start at the Arte Antwerp flagship and the Ultimate Sneaker Store, then check the smaller independent shops along the same street.

facets.title: Studded leather belt
facets.subtitle: Wide leather belt with pyramid studs and a heavy roller buckle.

curator.name: Fleur Iven
curator.tone: Innovative fashion + Antwerp in monochrome.
curator.antwerpTips: Start at Labels Inc. for designer consignment, then visit the Ann Demeulemeester store and the MoMu shop. The Nationalestraat boutiques carry the monochrome, deconstructed pieces this look is built on.

facets.title: Faded band tee
facets.subtitle: Sun-faded single-stitch band tee with cracked front print.

curator.name: Vincent D.
curator.tone: Vibrant, loud & structural streetwear style
curator.bio: Streetwear with an artist's eye. Loud, structural silhouettes pulled from Antwerp's boutiques and skate spots.
curator.quote: If it does not make you look twice on the street, it is not worth wearing.
curator.antwerpTips: Kammenstraat is the main street for streetwear. Start at the Arte Antwerp flagship and the Ultimate Sneaker Store, then check the smaller independent shops along the same street.

facets.title: Cowboy boots
facets.subtitle: Tan tooled-leather western boots with a stacked Cuban heel.

curator.name: Vincent D.
curator.tone: Vibrant, loud & structural streetwear style
curator.bio: Streetwear with an artist's eye. Loud, structural silhouettes pulled from Antwerp's boutiques and skate spots.
curator.quote: If it does not make you look twice on the street, it is not worth wearing.
curator.antwerpTips: Kammenstraat is the main street for streetwear. Start at the Arte Antwerp flagship and the Ultimate Sneaker Store, then check the smaller independent shops along the same street.

curator.name: Noor B.
curator.tone: Scrappy thrift enthusiast seeking stories in style pieces!
curator.bio: A thrift devotee digging the city's flea markets and second-hand rails for pieces with a real story.
curator.quote: The best pieces already have a past. I just give them a second act.
curator.antwerpTips: For everyday vintage, go to Think Twice and Episode on Kammenstraat. For older and rarer pieces, walk along Kloosterstraat. Visit on a Friday for the Vrijdagmarkt flea market, or a Sunday for the brocante market at Sint-Annaplein.

curator.name: Lexx
curator.tone: Fun-loving maximalist with a penchant for the night scene
curator.bio: Bold, print-driven maximalist. I love eclectic looks that come alive after dark - the perfect time to discover Antwerp!
curator.quote: More print, more colour, more night. Restraint is overrated.
curator.antwerpTips: Walk down Nationalestraat into the Zuid district. See the Dries Van Noten boutique and the HOST concept store, then look for the independent ateliers and jewellery studios in between.

facets.title: Dark Avant-Garde
facets.subtitle: Monochrome, deconstructed, architectural — the Antwerp Six legacy.

curator.name: Vincent D.
curator.tone: Vibrant, loud & structural streetwear style
curator.bio: Streetwear with an artist's eye. Loud, structural silhouettes pulled from Antwerp's boutiques and skate spots.
curator.quote: If it does not make you look twice on the street, it is not worth wearing.
curator.antwerpTips: Kammenstraat is the main street for streetwear. Start at the Arte Antwerp flagship and the Ultimate Sneaker Store, then check the smaller independent shops along the same street.

facets.title: Streetwear
facets.subtitle: Oversized, branded, hip-hop-rooted street style.

curator.name: Lexx
curator.tone: Fun-loving maximalist with a penchant for the night scene
curator.bio: Bold, print-driven maximalist. I love eclectic looks that come alive after dark - the perfect time to discover Antwerp!
curator.quote: More print, more colour, more night. Restraint is overrated.

facets.title: Eclectic & Print-Driven
facets.subtitle: Layered prints, rich colour, craft and pattern.

curator.name: Noor B.
curator.tone: Scrappy thrift enthusiast seeking stories in style pieces!
curator.bio: A thrift devotee digging the city's flea markets and second-hand rails for pieces with a real story.
curator.quote: The best pieces already have a past. I just give them a second act.
curator.antwerpTips: For everyday vintage, go to Think Twice and Episode on Kammenstraat. For older and rarer pieces, walk along Kloosterstraat. Visit on a Friday for the Vrijdagmarkt flea market, or a Sunday for the brocante market at Sint-Annaplein.

facets.title: Conscious Slow Fashion
facets.subtitle: Ethical, durable, quiet design.

curator.name: Maaike V.
curator.tone: Slowing fashion down in a rapidly changing world.
curator.bio: Slow, conscious fashion. I focus on ethical labels and natural materials, chosen to last. There is no Planet B.
curator.quote: I would rather own five things I really wear than a closet full of things I forget.
curator.antwerpTips: Supergoods and the sustainable section of the HOST concept store stock ethical labels. For affordable second-hand, the Kringwinkel charity shops have many locations across the city.

facets.title: Curated Vintage & Era-Specific
facets.subtitle: Second-hand and decade-loyal pieces.

curator.name: Senne V.
curator.tone: Impeccable tailoring and formal wear, made for movement.
curator.bio: Tailored sportswear with a luxe, athletic edge. Structured pieces that move and last.
curator.quote: A tracksuit should hold its shape for ten years. That is not a high standard.
curator.antwerpTips: This look traces back to Dirk Bikkembergs and his sport couture. See his pieces in the Antwerp Six collection at the MoMu fashion museum, then browse the designer shops around Meir and Nationalestraat for tailored, technical fashion.

facets.subtitle: Upcycled patchwork canvas tote, one of a kind.

curator.name: Vincent D.
curator.tone: Vibrant, loud & structural streetwear style
curator.bio: Streetwear with an artist's eye. Loud, structural silhouettes pulled from Antwerp's boutiques and skate spots.
curator.quote: If it does not make you look twice on the street, it is not worth wearing.
curator.antwerpTips: Kammenstraat is the main street for streetwear. Start at the Arte Antwerp flagship and the Ultimate Sneaker Store, then check the smaller independent shops along the same street.

facets.title: Vintage sports jersey
facets.subtitle: Mesh-panel football jersey, bold all-over print.

curator.name: Senne V.
curator.tone: Impeccable tailoring and formal wear, made for movement.
curator.bio: Tailored sportswear with a luxe, athletic edge. Structured pieces that move and last.
curator.quote: A tracksuit should hold its shape for ten years. That is not a high standard.
curator.antwerpTips: This look traces back to Dirk Bikkembergs and his sport couture. See his pieces in the Antwerp Six collection at the MoMu fashion museum, then browse the designer shops around Meir and Nationalestraat for tailored, technical fashion.

facets.title: Studded denim vest
facets.subtitle: Cut-off denim vest covered in studs and patches.

curator.name: Fleur Iven
curator.tone: Innovative fashion + Antwerp in monochrome.
curator.antwerpTips: Start at Labels Inc. for designer consignment, then visit the Ann Demeulemeester store and the MoMu shop. The Nationalestraat boutiques carry the monochrome, deconstructed pieces this look is built on.

facets.title: Embroidered crewneck
facets.subtitle: Heavy crewneck with the signature heart embroidery.

curator.name: Lexx
curator.tone: Fun-loving maximalist with a penchant for the night scene
curator.bio: Bold, print-driven maximalist. I love eclectic looks that come alive after dark - the perfect time to discover Antwerp!
curator.quote: More print, more colour, more night. Restraint is overrated.
curator.antwerpTips: Walk down Nationalestraat into the Zuid district. See the Dries Van Noten boutique and the HOST concept store, then look for the independent ateliers and jewellery studios in between.

facets.title: Fair-trade chinos
facets.subtitle: Organic cotton chinos made under fair-trade terms.

curator.name: Maaike V.
curator.tone: Slowing fashion down in a rapidly changing world.
curator.bio: Slow, conscious fashion. I focus on ethical labels and natural materials, chosen to last. There is no Planet B.
curator.quote: I would rather own five things I really wear than a closet full of things I forget.
curator.antwerpTips: Supergoods and the sustainable section of the HOST concept store stock ethical labels. For affordable second-hand, the Kringwinkel charity shops have many locations across the city.

facets.title: Designer leather tote
facets.subtitle: Structured leather tote, consigned in great condition.

curator.name: Noor B.
curator.tone: Scrappy thrift enthusiast seeking stories in style pieces!
curator.bio: A thrift devotee digging the city's flea markets and second-hand rails for pieces with a real story.
curator.quote: The best pieces already have a past. I just give them a second act.
curator.antwerpTips: For everyday vintage, go to Think Twice and Episode on Kammenstraat. For older and rarer pieces, walk along Kloosterstraat. Visit on a Friday for the Vrijdagmarkt flea market, or a Sunday for the brocante market at Sint-Annaplein.
